replies.author: @RoamPlanet-4K
replies.text: This is such a profound and crucial question. The documentary beautifully captures how these communities have coexisted with nature for thousands of years, relying on ancient wisdom like the 'Qanat' water systems or seasonal migrations. It would be a tragedy of historic proportions if our relentless search for resources destroyed these living sanctuaries of human heritage. I truly share your hope that global awareness will help shield them from such exploitation, allowing their peace and beautiful land to endure.

comments.author: @tankamsiong2736
comments.text: how is the water supply system

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: Great question! Villages like this rely on “qanats” — gently sloping underground tunnels, sometimes miles long, that carry mountain groundwater down by gravity alone, no pumps needed. Some have run for over 2,000 years. A genuinely ingenious answer to life in a dry landscape.

comments.author: @atassm5772

comments.author: @duckytips-com
comments.text: Such a beautiful and peaceful look at Iran’s village life  🌄🏡 The mountains, old homes, traditions, and calm daily moments make this documentary feel so real and human. Amazing 4k journey, really enjoyed it! ✨👏🌍

replies.author: @duckytips-com
replies.text: @PlanetVista4K

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: So glad you enjoyed this journey into Iran's beautiful village life! It was truly special to capture these moments.

comments.author: @Толганай-и4п
comments.text: Вот молодцы живут своей земле и не захватывают чужие земли как Россия и каждые метры земли  умывают и живут даже вулканических образовании ! А Россия 68 % пустуют и есть где жить и развиваться им мало и мало земли и хватает где есть подземные богатство и благородные почвы проливая крови других народов , кощунственная страна  Россия ! Люди живут даже камнях
